channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for HerCyberTracks 2026, a high-impact international programme designed to empower women professionals in cybersecurity with advanced skills, global exposure, and leadership opportunities. This initiative is funded by the Federal Foreign Office of Germany and the European Union, and co-implemented by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with the United Nations […]

The post HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for HerCyberTracks 2026, a high-impact international programme designed to empower women professionals in cybersecurity with advanced skills, global exposure, and leadership opportunities.

This initiative is funded by the Federal Foreign Office of Germany and the European Union, and co-implemented by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and the Latin America and Caribbean Cyber Competence Centre (LAC4).

About the Programme

HerCyberTracks is a five-month blended learning programme that combines virtual training, self-paced learning, mentorship, and in-person regional sessions.

It is designed to support women working in cybersecurity-related roles to accelerate their careers, deepen technical expertise, and build strong international networks.

Programme Structure

Participants will engage in:

Live online sessions with industry experts
Self-paced learning modules
In-person regional training sessions
Personalised mentorship
Inspirational webinars and keynote sessions

This hybrid format ensures both flexibility and immersive learning experiences.

Specialised Tracks

Applicants can choose one of four tailored tracks:

Policy and Diplomacy
Incident Response
Criminal Justice
Cyber or AI

Each track is designed to provide practical knowledge and leadership skills relevant to the evolving cybersecurity landscape.

What You’ll Gain

Participants will benefit from:

Advanced cybersecurity and policy skills
Access to global experts and mentors
International networking opportunities
Exposure to cross-regional collaboration
Leadership development to influence policy and systems

Who Should Apply

This programme targets women professionals working in:

Public sector institutions
Critical infrastructure organisations
Cybersecurity, policy, or related technical fields

Eligible Regions

Applications are open to candidates from:

African Union member states
Western Balkans
Eastern Partnership countries (selected)
Latin America and the Caribbean
Indo-Pacific region

Programme Duration

Duration: 5 months (2026 cohort)

Application Deadline

Applications close on April 19, 2026.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Training Programs from OFY, Click here.

The post HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Comprehensive Security Fellowship 2027–2028 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers & Practitioners
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Fellowship on “Comprehensive Security for a Changing World: Building Strategic and Technological Resilience”—a prestigious programme designed for professionals and researchers working on global security, international relations, and resilience. Hosted in Bonn, Germany, this fellowship provides a unique platform to develop innovative ideas addressing today’s evolving geopolitical challenges. About the […]

The post Comprehensive Security Fellowship 2027–2028 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers & Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Fellowship on “Comprehensive Security for a Changing World: Building Strategic and Technological Resilience”—a prestigious programme designed for professionals and researchers working on global security, international relations, and resilience.

Hosted in Bonn, Germany, this fellowship provides a unique platform to develop innovative ideas addressing today’s evolving geopolitical challenges.

About the Fellowship

This programme brings together experts from diverse backgrounds to explore pressing issues in international politics, security, and technological resilience.

Participants will have the opportunity to work on forward-thinking projects that contribute to academic research and real-world policy solutions.

Fellowship Tracks

Applicants can choose between two distinct tracks depending on their background and goals:

Academic Track

Designed for researchers and scholars, this track focuses on developing research-driven projects that engage with current academic debates in security and international relations.

Practitioner’s Track

Tailored for professionals, this track emphasizes policy-oriented projects grounded in real-world experience and practical application.

What You’ll Gain

This fellowship offers a strong platform for professional and academic growth.

Key benefits include:

Opportunity to develop impactful research or policy projects
Engagement with global experts and peers
Exposure to international security and policy discussions
Access to a collaborative and intellectually stimulating environment
Enhanced career opportunities in academia, policy, or international organizations

Programme Details

Location: Bonn, Germany
Duration: April 2027 – March 2028

Why You Should Apply

As global challenges become more complex, there is a growing need for innovative thinking in security and resilience.

This fellowship provides the space, resources, and network to develop ideas that can influence policy and academic discourse on a global scale.

Application Deadline

Applications close on May 31, 2026.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post Comprehensive Security Fellowship 2027–2028 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers & Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective 2026: Funding & Tech Support for Women-Led Organisations in Africa
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for Cohort 2 of the A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective (GAIC)—a unique programme designed to support women-led organisations across sub-Saharan Africa in scaling their impact through technology. This initiative is co-led by Code for Africa (CfA) and Women at the Table, with support from the International Development Research Centre […]

The post A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective 2026: Funding & Tech Support for Women-Led Organisations in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for Cohort 2 of the A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective (GAIC)—a unique programme designed to support women-led organisations across sub-Saharan Africa in scaling their impact through technology.

This initiative is co-led by Code for Africa (CfA) and Women at the Table, with support from the International Development Research Centre (IDRC) and the FCDO Services under the Artificial Intelligence for Development (AI4D) programme.

About the Programme

The Gender & AI Innovation Collective (GAIC) is designed to help women-led organisations build and scale tech-driven solutions that address critical challenges affecting women and girls.

Rather than just funding ideas, the programme provides hands-on support to develop, refine, and scale technology solutions that can drive real impact.

What the Programme Offers

Selected organisations will benefit from a structured support system that combines innovation, mentorship, and technology development.

Key benefits include:

Technical support to build and scale digital solutions
Access to expert mentorship and advisory
Opportunities to strengthen organisational capacity
Collaboration with a network of like-minded organisations
Support in leveraging AI and technology for social impact

Focus Areas

The programme is looking for organisations actively working to address:

Gender-based violence
Economic inequality
Healthcare access
Education for women and girls

Solutions that use or can integrate technology—especially AI—are strongly encouraged.

Programme Details

Application Window: March 5 – April 25, 2026
Programme Start Date: May 13, 2026
Format: Fully online
Time Commitment: Approximately 4 hours per week

Why You Should Apply

Women-led organisations often face barriers in accessing technology, funding, and scaling opportunities. This programme is designed to bridge that gap by providing the tools and support needed to expand impact.

If your organisation is working to improve the lives of women and girls, this is a powerful opportunity to grow your solution and reach more communities.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective 2026: Funding & Tech Support for Women-Led Organisations in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Peter J. Rappa Resilient & Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Peter J. Rappa Resilient and Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026, a unique summer programme designed for students passionate about sustainability, climate adaptation, and coastal resilience. This impactful fellowship is supported by the University of Hawai‘i Sea Grant College Program and offers participants the chance to work on real-world projects across […]

The post Peter J. Rappa Resilient & Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Peter J. Rappa Resilient and Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026, a unique summer programme designed for students passionate about sustainability, climate adaptation, and coastal resilience.

This impactful fellowship is supported by the University of Hawai‘i Sea Grant College Program and offers participants the chance to work on real-world projects across Molokaʻi and Oʻahu, Hawaii, United States.

About the Fellowship

The Rappa Fellowship is a 10-week summer programme that equips students with hands-on experience in building sustainable coastal communities.

Fellows work on projects that bridge the gap between research and practical solutions, contributing to climate resilience, coastal planning, and community development.

What You’ll Gain

This fellowship offers both financial support and practical experience in sustainability-focused work.

Key benefits include:

A stipend of $8,000
Hands-on experience in climate adaptation and coastal resilience
Exposure to real-world sustainability and planning projects
Opportunities to integrate traditional knowledge into modern solutions
Professional development in research, communication, and policy

Focus Areas

Fellows will work across key areas such as:

Sustainable coastal communities
Climate adaptation and resilience
Coastal smart growth and hazard mitigation
Environmental communication and planning

Programme Details

Duration: 10 weeks (June – August 2026)
Location: Molokaʻi and Oʻahu, Hawai‘i

Who Should Apply

This programme is ideal for students and recent graduates interested in sustainability, environmental science, or coastal development.

Applicants must:

Be full-time undergraduate or graduate students, or recent graduates
Be a U.S. citizen or national
Demonstrate interest in sustainability, climate, or coastal systems

Application Requirements

Applicants are required to submit:

Goal Statement (max 2 pages)
Resume (max 2 pages)
Unofficial transcripts
Contact information for two references

Key Dates

Informational Webinar: March 27, 2026 @ 11 AM HST
Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 (5 PM HST)

How to Apply

Start your application here.

Learn more.

For More Fellowship Opportunities from OFY, Click Here.

The post Peter J. Rappa Resilient & Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Students and early-career professionals interning on Capitol Hill often face significant financial challenges, especially due to the high cost of living in Washington, D.C. To address this barrier and promote inclusivity, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation (WCSF) is offering its Summer 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship—a funding opportunity designed to support women pursuing internships in the […]

The post 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and early-career professionals interning on Capitol Hill often face significant financial challenges, especially due to the high cost of living in Washington, D.C. To address this barrier and promote inclusivity, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation (WCSF) is offering its Summer 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship—a funding opportunity designed to support women pursuing internships in the U.S. legislative environment.

This scholarship not only provides financial assistance but also opens doors to valuable networking opportunities and mentorship from professionals serving on the WCSF Board. It is a strategic initiative aimed at increasing the representation and participation of women—especially those from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds—in the legislative workforce.

About the Scholarship

The WCSF Scholarship is dedicated to broadening the voices and experiences of women on Capitol Hill. Recognizing that internships often serve as a critical entry point into full-time roles in government and policy, the program seeks to strengthen the pipeline by supporting women at the very beginning of their careers.

A key focus of the scholarship is to ensure that women from middle- and low-income backgrounds are not excluded from these opportunities due to financial constraints. By reducing the burden of living and working in Washington, D.C., WCSF enables more women to contribute their perspectives and expertise to the legislative process.

Scholarship Benefits

Recipients of the WCSF Scholarship will gain:

  • Financial support to help cover expenses during their Capitol Hill internship
  • Professional networking opportunities with leaders and policymakers
  • Career mentorship and guidance from members of the WCSF Board
  • Exposure to the inner workings of the U.S. legislative system

Application Deadline and Process

  • Application Deadline: April 15, 2026 (11:59 PM EST)
  • Interview Notification: Finalists will be contacted to schedule interviews with the selection committee
  • Final Award Announcement: No later than May 24, 2026

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early submission is highly encouraged to increase your chances of selection.

Why This Scholarship Matters

Internships on Capitol Hill are often a stepping stone to influential careers in government, policy, and public service. However, without financial support, many qualified candidates—particularly women from underrepresented backgrounds—may be unable to pursue these opportunities.

The WCSF Scholarship plays a crucial role in leveling the playing field, ensuring that talent and passion—not financial limitations—determine who gets a seat at the table in shaping public policy.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must:

  • Have applied for an internship with:
    • A Member, Delegate, or Resident Commissioner of Congress, OR
    • A U.S. Senate or House committee, caucus, or leadership office in Washington, D.C.
  • Provide a confirmation letter of the internship before receiving funding
  • Identify as a woman
  • Be at least 18 years old
  • Hold a diploma from an accredited high school

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on several important factors:

  • Demonstrated financial need, including expenses related to the internship
  • Alignment between the internship and the applicant’s academic and career goals
  • Applications for internships with significant responsibility and impact
  • Overall quality of application materials, including resume, essays, and references
  • Academic performance and track record

Terms and Conditions

Recipients of the scholarship are required to:

  • Complete the full internship as described in their application
  • Provide accurate and truthful information, particularly regarding financial need

Failure to meet these requirements may result in forfeiture or repayment of scholarship funds. Exceptions will only be made in rare and exceptional circumstances, as determined by the WCSF Board of Directors.

Additionally, the scholarship funds are non-transferable and can only be used for the internship specified in the application.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications before the deadline of April 15, 2026. Since applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, applying early is strongly recommended.

APPLY HERE

By supporting aspiring women leaders at the start of their careers, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation is helping to build a more inclusive, representative, and effective legislative workforce.

Discover more similar opportunities here. 

The post 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: FAO Girls in ICT Day 2026 E-Mentorship on AI in Agrifood Systems
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
In celebration of International Girls in ICT Day 2026, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has announced an exciting global opportunity for young women to participate in a virtual e-mentorship program focused on artificial intelligence (AI) and its role in agrifood systems. This initiative is designed to bring together aspiring young […]

The post Apply Now: FAO Girls in ICT Day 2026 E-Mentorship on AI in Agrifood Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In celebration of International Girls in ICT Day 2026, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has announced an exciting global opportunity for young women to participate in a virtual e-mentorship program focused on artificial intelligence (AI) and its role in agrifood systems. This initiative is designed to bring together aspiring young women, experienced mentors, and global stakeholders to engage in meaningful discussions about the future of technology and its impact on food systems worldwide.

About the Initiative

The E-Mentorship program is part of FAO’s broader efforts to address the digital gender divide by equipping girls and young women with the knowledge, confidence, and networks needed to thrive in technology-driven fields. Through this global online event, participants will explore both the challenges and opportunities that AI presents in transforming agrifood systems.

Agrifood systems are increasingly being shaped by digital innovations, and AI is playing a critical role in improving agricultural productivity, sustainability, and resilience. However, women and girls remain underrepresented in these technological advancements. This mentorship program seeks to bridge that gap by encouraging greater participation and ensuring that girls are not only beneficiaries of innovation but also active contributors to its development.

Event Theme and Objectives

The 2026 edition of the event will take place on April 23 under the theme:

“AI for Development: Girls Shaping the Digital Future.”

This theme underscores the importance of inclusive innovation and highlights the vital role that girls and young women play in accelerating the adoption and ethical use of AI technologies. It emphasizes that empowering girls in ICT is not only a matter of equality but also a necessity for sustainable global development.

The key objectives of the event include:

  • Promoting awareness of AI applications in agrifood systems
  • Encouraging girls to pursue careers and opportunities in ICT and related fields
  • Providing mentorship and role models from diverse professional backgrounds
  • Facilitating knowledge exchange between participants and experts
  • Strengthening global efforts to close the digital gender gap

Structure of the E-Mentorship Session

A central component of this initiative is a two-hour virtual mentorship session tailored specifically for young women interested in AI and agrifood systems. This interactive session will feature:

  • Expert Mentors: Professionals from the private sector, academia, and United Nations agencies will participate as mentors. These individuals bring a wealth of experience in AI, agriculture, and digital innovation.
  • Career Insights: Mentors will share their personal career journeys, including the challenges they faced, the milestones they achieved, and the lessons they learned along the way.
  • Aspirational Guidance: Participants will gain valuable advice on how to navigate educational and career pathways in technology and agrifood sectors.
  • Interactive Q&A Session: Attendees will have the opportunity to ask questions, seek guidance, and engage directly with experts.

The session is designed to be highly engaging and participatory, ensuring that attendees not only listen but actively contribute to discussions.

Certification and Participation Benefits

All participants who successfully attend and complete the mentorship session will receive a certificate of participation. This certificate serves as recognition of their engagement and commitment to learning about AI applications in agrifood systems.

Beyond certification, participants will benefit from:

  • Exposure to global perspectives on AI and agriculture
  • Opportunities to network with professionals and peers
  • Increased awareness of career pathways in emerging technological fields
  • Enhanced confidence to participate in digital innovation spaces

Eligibility Criteria

FAO invites applications from young women around the world who meet the following requirements:

  • Must be female and aged between 18 and 25 years
  • Demonstrated interest in technology, agriculture, or the intersection of both
  • Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from a post-secondary institution (university or vocational program)
  • Academic background in STEM or agriculture is advantageous but not mandatory
  • Strong motivation to learn about AI applications in agrifood systems
  • Commitment to attend the full mentorship session and actively participate in all activities
  • Working proficiency in English
  • Willingness to provide feedback after the session to support the improvement of future programs

Application Process and Deadline

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply by April 5, 2026. Early registration is recommended due to the global nature of the program and the potential for high demand.

Click here to Register

Applicants should ensure they meet all eligibility criteria and are available to attend the full session on April 23, 2026.

Advancing Inclusion in the Digital Age

This initiative reflects FAO’s commitment to fostering inclusive digital transformation in agrifood systems. By investing in the next generation of female leaders in ICT, the organization aims to create a more equitable and innovative future.

Programs like this not only provide knowledge and mentorship but also inspire confidence and ambition among young women. As AI continues to reshape industries worldwide, ensuring that girls are equipped to participate meaningfully in this transformation is essential.

The E-Mentorship for Girls in ICT Day 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for young women to gain insights, build networks, and take a step forward in shaping the digital future of agrifood systems.

Discover more similar opportunities here

 

The post Apply Now: FAO Girls in ICT Day 2026 E-Mentorship on AI in Agrifood Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Unlock New Markets: Apply for the AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
African agribusinesses are at the heart of the continent’s economic transformation. If you are an export-ready Small or Medium Enterprise (SME) operating within the agri-food value chain, this is your opportunity to scale beyond borders and tap into regional and international markets through the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA). The AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking […]

The post Unlock New Markets: Apply for the AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
African agribusinesses are at the heart of the continent’s economic transformation. If you are an export-ready Small or Medium Enterprise (SME) operating within the agri-food value chain, this is your opportunity to scale beyond borders and tap into regional and international markets through the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA).

The AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026 is designed to help ambitious businesses expand their reach, strengthen their export capabilities, and build meaningful trade partnerships across Africa and with global markets like Switzerland.

What Is the Programme About?

This initiative is a targeted business support programme aimed at empowering agribusiness SMEs to leverage AfCFTA opportunities. A total of 30 high-potential businesses will be selected through a competitive process to receive tailored support that enhances their readiness for cross-border trade.

Key Benefits of the Programme

Selected participants will gain access to a comprehensive support package, including:

  • Specialized Training on AfCFTA Opportunities
    Understand how to navigate and benefit from Africa’s largest free trade agreement.
  • Market Expansion Support
    Position your business to enter new regional and international markets.
  • Packaging and Labelling Assistance
    Improve your product presentation to meet international standards.
  • Business Matchmaking & Buyer Connections
    Engage directly with potential buyers through curated B2B sessions.
  • Access to Trade Fairs and Market Linkage Events
    Showcase your products and connect with key industry players.
  • Tailored Coaching by Sales Experts
    Develop effective go-to-market strategies with expert guidance.
  • Participation in AfCFTA Flagship Events
    Gain visibility and network at high-level continental trade events.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for agribusiness SMEs that:

  • Operate within agriculture or food value chains
  • Are export-ready or preparing to scale internationally
  • Are committed to actively participating in programme activities
  • Seek to explore new trade and partnership opportunities

Women-led and youth-led enterprises are strongly encouraged to apply.

Programme Impact

By participating in this programme, SMEs will not only strengthen their competitiveness but also gain the tools and networks needed to thrive in the evolving African trade landscape. With AfCFTA unlocking a market of over 1.3 billion people, this initiative positions your business at the forefront of continental trade integration.

Application Deadline

All interested applicants must submit their Expression of Interest before: 6 April 2026

Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is highly recommended.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

Confidentiality Notice

All submitted information will be handled with strict confidentiality and used solely for analytical and programme evaluation purposes.

Final Thoughts

This is more than just a training programme—it is a gateway to scaling your agribusiness across Africa and beyond. If you are ready to grow, connect, and compete on a larger stage, this opportunity could be your next big step.

The post Unlock New Markets: Apply for the AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 in San Jose: High-Paying Opportunity for U.S. University Students
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 is a highly competitive, full-time internship opportunity designed for undergraduate students in the United States who are eager to gain hands-on experience in product management within a global technology company. This program stands out for its strong emphasis on real-world product development, cross-functional collaboration, and the integration of AI-powered […]

The post eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 in San Jose: High-Paying Opportunity for U.S. University Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 is a highly competitive, full-time internship opportunity designed for undergraduate students in the United States who are eager to gain hands-on experience in product management within a global technology company. This program stands out for its strong emphasis on real-world product development, cross-functional collaboration, and the integration of AI-powered tools in modern product workflows.

With a structured 6-month duration and a generous hourly pay of up to $47, this co-op provides a rare opportunity to work directly on products used by millions of users worldwide while building critical skills in product strategy, execution, and analytics.

About the eBay Product Management Co-op

This co-op program is tailored for emerging talent, especially Gen Z students, who are interested in building digital products for a global audience. Participants are embedded within product teams and collaborate closely with engineering, design, research, and data professionals.

The program emphasizes:

  • AI-enabled product discovery and decision-making
  • Real-world product lifecycle exposure
  • Ownership of meaningful product outcomes
  • Mentorship from experienced product leaders

By the end of the program, participants are expected to deliver a comprehensive presentation showcasing their contributions, learnings, and strategic recommendations.

Key Responsibilities

During the co-op, selected candidates will:

Product Discovery and Research

  • Conduct user research and analyze customer insights
  • Perform competitive analysis
  • Use data to identify opportunities and define problems

Product Development and Execution

  • Define product requirements and user stories
  • Collaborate with engineering and design teams
  • Participate in Agile or Scrum-based workflows

AI and Innovation

  • Use AI-powered tools for prototyping, research, and iteration
  • Accelerate workflows using automation and analytics tools

Performance Tracking and Analysis

  • Monitor product performance metrics
  • Evaluate post-launch results
  • Generate insights for continuous improvement

Communication and Collaboration

  • Work cross-functionally with multiple teams
  • Present findings and updates to stakeholders
  • Align product goals across departments

Eligibility Criteria

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to:

  • Undergraduate students currently enrolled full-time in a U.S.-accredited university
  • Students graduating between December 2026 and Summer 2027

Eligible Fields of Study

Applicants should be pursuing degrees in:

  • Product Management
  • Business Administration
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Computer Science
  • Engineering
  • Economics
  • Design or Industrial Design
  • Related disciplines

Who Should Apply

This co-op is ideal for:

  • Students passionate about product management and technology
  • Individuals interested in building user-centric digital experiences
  • Candidates looking to gain experience in a major global tech company
  • Those who want to work at the intersection of AI, data, and product innovation

Apply here

Countries Eligible

  • Only students enrolled in accredited universities within the United States
  • Must have legal authorization to work in the U.S.

This opportunity is not open to international applicants without U.S. work authorization.

Work Authorization Requirements (Very Important)

This role has strict eligibility conditions:

  • Applicants must have U.S. work authorization
  • Visa sponsorship is NOT provided
  • Students on F1, CPT, OPT, or requiring H1B sponsorship are NOT eligible

Required Qualifications

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Experience (academic, internship, or project-based) in product management, technology, analytics, or consulting
  • Familiarity with AI tools such as generative AI, data analysis platforms, or automation tools
  • Strong analytical thinking and data-driven decision-making skills
  • Excellent communication skills (written and verbal)
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment
  • A growth mindset with curiosity and adaptability

Preferred Qualifications

While not mandatory, the following will strengthen your application:

  • Experience with Agile or Scrum methodologies
  • Understanding of digital marketplaces or platform-based businesses
  • Basic technical knowledge (APIs, backend systems, architectures)
  • Experience solving ambiguous or complex problems
  • Cross-functional collaboration experience

Benefits of the Program

Financial Benefits

  • Competitive hourly pay: $42–$47
  • Full-time work experience over 6 months

Career Development

  • Exposure to real-world product management
  • Mentorship from industry professionals
  • Hands-on experience with AI-driven product tools

Professional Impact

  • Opportunity to contribute to products used globally
  • Build a strong portfolio project
  • Gain experience highly valued by top tech companies

Location and Work Setup

  • Primary Location: San Jose, California
  • Work Mode: Hybrid (combination of in-office and remote work)
  • Relocation: Candidates must be willing to relocate if not already in the area

Important Dates

  • Interview Day: April 10, 2026 (mandatory availability required)
  • Interview Duration: 1.5 hours (virtual)
  • Application Policy: Only one application per candidate is allowed
  • Start Date: June 15, 2026
  • End Date: December 11, 2026

Applicants must be available for the scheduled interview day. No alternative dates or rescheduling options are provided, making this a strict and critical deadline.

Deadline

8th April 2026

The eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 is a premier opportunity for students aiming to break into product management. With strong compensation, hands-on experience, and exposure to cutting-edge AI tools, this program offers both professional growth and real-world impact.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 in San Jose: High-Paying Opportunity for U.S. University Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FULLY FUNDED OML MENA FELLOWSHIP 2026: Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in the Middle East and North Africa
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are a young leader passionate about creating real impact in your community, the One Million Leaders (OML) MENA Fellowship 2026 is one of the most transformative fully funded opportunities you can apply for right now. This prestigious leadership program is designed to equip the next generation of changemakers with practical skills, global networks, […]

The post FULLY FUNDED OML MENA FELLOWSHIP 2026: Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in the Middle East and North Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a young leader passionate about creating real impact in your community, the One Million Leaders (OML) MENA Fellowship 2026 is one of the most transformative fully funded opportunities you can apply for right now. This prestigious leadership program is designed to equip the next generation of changemakers with practical skills, global networks, and hands-on experience to drive sustainable change across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

What is the OML MENA Fellowship 2026?

The OML MENA Fellowship is a highly impactful leadership development program designed to empower young leaders with skills in sustainability, social innovation, and ethical leadership. It is part of a global initiative aiming to train one million leaders by 2030.

The program focuses on building leaders who are not just thinkers but active problem-solvers working on real-world challenges in their communities. Participants engage in structured training, mentorship, and project implementation phases that translate ideas into measurable impact.

Why You Should Apply for the OML MENA Fellowship

The OML Fellowship is more than just a training program—it is a life-changing leadership experience. Here is what makes it stand out:

1. Fully Funded Participation – Selected fellows receive access to all program components at no cost, including training, mentorship, and resources.

2. World-Class Leadership Training – Participants undergo intensive training based on five core pillars:

  • Sustainability Essentials
  • Ethical Leadership
  • Social Innovation
  • Personal Development
  • Community Impact

3. Mentorship and Global Networking – Fellows connect with:

  • Global mentors
  • Industry experts
  • Like-minded young leaders across the MENA region and beyond

4. Real Impact Through Projects – Participants design and implement community-based projects addressing real social or environmental challenges. Some projects may even receive micro-grant funding for execution.

5. Alumni and Long-Term Opportunities – Graduates join a global alumni network that offers:

  • Continued mentorship
  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Access to future funding and programs

Program Structure: What to Expect

The OML MENA Fellowship is structured into four major phases:

Phase 1: Intensive Training – Fellows undergo online training sessions focused on leadership and sustainability.

Phase 2: Leadership Challenge – Participants train and mentor a group of young leaders (“Champions”), multiplying their impact.

Phase 3: Act-for-Change Project – Fellows implement real-life projects that address key issues in their communities.

Phase 4: Graduation & Alumni Network – Successful participants graduate and join a global leadership network.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

The OML MENA Fellowship is specifically designed for young changemakers in the MENA region.

Eligible Categories of Applicants:

  • Young leaders
  • Social entrepreneurs
  • Community organizers
  • Sustainability advocates
  • Students and early-career professionals

Basic Requirements:

  • Age: Typically 18 to 40 years
  • Strong interest in social impact, sustainability, or community development
  • Demonstrated leadership or involvement in a project or initiative
  • Willingness to mentor and lead others
  • Commitment to complete the program
  • Access to internet for virtual sessions

Eligible Countries and Regions

The fellowship is open to applicants from the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region, including but not limited to:

  • Egypt
  • Morocco
  • Tunisia
  • Algeria
  • Jordan
  • Lebanon
  • Saudi Arabia
  • United Arab Emirates
  • فلسطين (Palestine)
  • Iraq
  • Sudan
  • Somalia
  • Yemen
  • Qatar
  • Kuwait

Applicants must either be citizens or residents of MENA countries.

Key Benefits of the OML MENA Fellowship 2026

Participating in this fellowship gives you access to:

  • Fully funded leadership training
  • Mentorship from global experts
  • Opportunity to lead and train other young leaders
  • Access to micro-grants for impactful projects
  • Exposure to international networks
  • Skill development in sustainability and innovation
  • Recognition as an OML Fellow

Why This Fellowship Matters in 2026

In today’s world, leadership is no longer just about authority—it is about impact, innovation, and sustainability. The OML MENA Fellowship aligns with global development goals by nurturing leaders who can:

  • Address climate challenges
  • Drive social innovation
  • Build inclusive communities
  • Empower future generations

This fellowship is particularly valuable for young people who want to move from ideas to action and become part of a global movement for change.

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

To apply for the OML MENA Fellowship 2026, follow these steps:

Step 1: Online Registration – Complete the official application form via the OML platform.

Step 2: Submit Project Details – Provide information about:

  • Your leadership experience
  • Your social impact work
  • Your vision for change

Step 3: Screening & Interviews – Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews.

Step 4: Final Selection – Successful applicants are admitted into the fellowship program.

Apply here

Deadline

20th April 2026 (GMT+2)

The OML MENA Fellowship 2026 is one of the most comprehensive and impactful fully funded leadership programs available for young people in the MENA region. It offers not just knowledge, but real-world experience, funding opportunities, and a powerful global network.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post FULLY FUNDED OML MENA FELLOWSHIP 2026: Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in the Middle East and North Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WILD Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Fellowship for Women Driving Social Impact
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The WILD Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious global leadership fellowship designed to empower women across the world to lead transformational social impact initiatives. This opportunity stands out as one of the most impactful global fellowships focused on equipping women leaders with the tools, networks, and platforms needed to influence change at local, regional, and global […]

The post WILD Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Fellowship for Women Driving Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The WILD Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious global leadership fellowship designed to empower women across the world to lead transformational social impact initiatives. This opportunity stands out as one of the most impactful global fellowships focused on equipping women leaders with the tools, networks, and platforms needed to influence change at local, regional, and global levels.

According to Official Fellowship Page, the program targets women at different career stages who are committed to advancing leadership, sustainability, and social change initiatives in their communities and beyond.

What is the WILD Fellowship 2026?

The WILD Fellowship is a high-impact global leadership program tailored specifically for women who are passionate about creating meaningful change. It brings together a diverse cohort of participants from across continents to engage in leadership forums, capacity-building sessions, and collaborative learning experiences.

This fellowship is structured to provide both online and in-person engagement opportunities, ensuring accessibility for participants worldwide while still offering immersive leadership experiences.

The program is unique because it does not only focus on academic or technical skills, but also emphasizes:

  • Leadership transformation
  • Personal growth and empowerment
  • Global networking and collaboration
  • Real-world impact through social initiatives

Participants become part of a powerful global network of women leaders committed to addressing pressing global challenges.

Fellowship Overview and Key Highlights

The WILD Fellowship 2026 offers a comprehensive experience that goes beyond traditional training programs. It combines mentorship, peer learning, and leadership development in a dynamic and inclusive environment.

Core Highlights of the Fellowship

  • Global leadership development program for women
  • Access to international forums and leadership events
  • Opportunity to connect with influential women leaders worldwide
  • Blended learning format (online and in-person sessions)
  • Focus on social impact, sustainability, and community leadership

The fellowship is particularly beneficial for individuals seeking to expand their leadership influence while working on projects that create real change.

Who Can Apply for the WILD Fellowship 2026?

The fellowship is open to a broad and diverse group of women leaders across the globe. It is intentionally inclusive, ensuring representation from different regions, sectors, and career stages.

Eligible Categories of Applicants

  • Early-career professionals
  • Mid-career professionals
  • Senior-level leaders
  • Social entrepreneurs
  • Community leaders and activists
  • Professionals working in NGOs, government, or private sector

Gender Requirement

  • The fellowship is specifically designed for women committed to leadership and social impact.

Eligible Countries and Regions

The WILD Fellowship is a global opportunity, meaning applicants can apply from:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Europe
  • North America
  • South America
  • Oceania

There are no strict geographical limitations, making it accessible to women from both developed and developing regions.

Required Qualifications and Eligibility Criteria

While the fellowship is inclusive, applicants must demonstrate strong leadership potential and a commitment to social change.

Key Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should:

  • Demonstrate a clear commitment to social impact or community development
  • Show evidence of leadership experience or potential
  • Be actively engaged in initiatives that contribute to positive change
  • Possess strong communication and collaboration skills
  • Be willing to participate fully in fellowship activities (online and/or in-person)

Unlike many academic programs, the WILD Fellowship places greater emphasis on impact and leadership rather than formal academic qualifications.

Benefits of the WILD Fellowship 2026

The fellowship offers a wide range of benefits that extend beyond financial support. It is designed to create long-term value for participants.

Professional and Leadership Benefits

  • Advanced leadership training and development
  • Exposure to global best practices in social impact
  • Opportunities to lead and collaborate on international initiatives

Networking Opportunities

  • Access to a global network of women leaders
  • Collaboration with changemakers from diverse sectors
  • Lifelong connections with fellowship alumni

Personal Growth

  • Enhanced confidence and leadership identity
  • Improved communication and strategic thinking skills
  • Personal transformation through mentorship and peer engagement

Why You Should Apply for the WILD Fellowship

The WILD Fellowship is not just another leadership program—it is a transformational journey for women who want to make a difference.

Here’s why this opportunity stands out:

  • It focuses on real-world impact, not just theory
  • It connects you with a global community of leaders
  • It enhances both personal and professional growth
  • It provides a platform to amplify your work and influence

For women in Africa, Asia, and other underrepresented regions, this fellowship offers a powerful opportunity to gain global visibility and recognition.

Application Process for the WILD Fellowship 2026

While specific steps may vary slightly, the general application process includes:

  1. Online Application Submission
    • Complete the official application form
  2. Personal Statement or Essays
    • Highlight leadership experience and social impact work
  3. Supporting Documents
    • May include CV, references, or project details
  4. Selection Process
    • Shortlisting and potential interviews

Applicants are encouraged to present a compelling narrative that showcases their passion, leadership journey, and vision for impact.

Apply here

Deadline

31st March 2026

The WILD Fellowship 2026 is one of the most impactful global fellowships for women seeking to scale their leadership and social impact initiatives.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post WILD Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Fellowship for Women Driving Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
